name: Standardization-Driven Interoperability Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Formalizing open-standard adoption requirements for identity systems → Systemic: 40% reduction in platform-specific authentication dependencies by 2029 (based on eIDAS2 compliance benchmarks) → Strategic: Creates enforceable technical boundaries for foreign platform influence in public services.
options: Mandate FIDO2/WebAuthn as the baseline for all public-sector identity integration
Develop a certified open-source identity proxy layer to decouple service providers from platform APIs
Implement blockchain-based audit trails for authentication event verification
review: Controls Technical Control vs. Institutional Resistance. Weakness: The options fail to consider the political feasibility of mandating open standards against entrenched platform interests.

name: Procurement-Linked Resilience Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Embedding fallback-path requirements in public tenders → Systemic: 25% increase in multi-platform identity solution bids by 2028 (based on EU procurement trend analysis) → Strategic: Institutionalizes platform-neutral design as a contractual obligation.
options: Require bid evaluation criteria that penalize exclusive reliance on Apple/Google authentication APIs
Create a public registry of certified platform-neutral identity providers
Link funding for digital services to compliance with multi-channel authentication standards
review: Controls Short-Term Compliance vs. Long-Term Flexibility. Weakness: The options understate the risk of regulatory capture by existing platform vendors in procurement processes.

name: Coalition-Enhanced Policy Advocacy Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Formalizing cross-sector stakeholder agreements on identity resilience → Systemic: 30% increase in parliamentary committee support for digital sovereignty initiatives by 2027 (based on Folketinget engagement metrics) → Strategic: Establishes a durable political mandate for platform-neutral reforms.
options: Create a multi-stakeholder governance body with voting rights proportional to service impact
Develop a standardized impact assessment framework for identity system dependencies
Launch a public awareness campaign framing platform neutrality as a democratic imperative
review: Controls Political Momentum vs. Technical Precision. Weakness: The options lack specific mechanisms to prevent co-optation by commercial interests during coalition formation.

name: Demonstrator-Driven Credibility Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Publishing verifiable technical proofs of platform-neutral authentication → Systemic: 50% increase in public-sector pilot programs adopting alternative identity models by 2028 (based on AltID adoption rates) → Strategic: Creates a de facto standard for fallback capabilities through empirical validation.
options: Develop a minimal viable proof-of-concept for browser-based FIDO2 authentication
Create a hardware token reference implementation compliant with ETSI EN 319 401 standards
Build a simulated identity provider environment with multi-platform API compatibility
review: Controls Technical Validation vs. Institutional Adoption. Weakness: The options don't address the challenge of translating technical proofs into procurement requirements.

name: Contingency-Preparedness Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Establishing formal fallback protocol documentation → Systemic: 60% improvement in emergency response readiness for identity system failures by 2029 (based on DRaaS metrics) → Strategic: Transforms identity infrastructure from a vulnerability into a resilience asset.
options: Implement mandatory quarterly stress tests for platform-independent authentication pathways
Create a national identity resilience certification program with tiered compliance levels
Develop a real-time monitoring dashboard for platform-specific authentication failure rates
review: Controls Immediate Resilience vs. Long-Term Adaptability. Weakness: The options fail to account for the resource constraints of public-sector agencies in maintaining contingency systems.

name: Regulatory Governance Framework Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Instituting mandatory compliance thresholds for platform-neutral identity protocols → Systemic: 35% increase in regulatory scrutiny of vendor-specific authentication models by 2028 (based on eIDAS2 enforcement metrics) → Strategic: Embeds digital sovereignty as a non-negotiable criterion in public-sector identity governance.
options: Establish a national regulatory sandbox for platform-neutral authentication pilots
Require public-sector agencies to submit dependency impact assessments for identity systems
Create a tiered certification process for identity providers based on vendor neutrality
review: Controls Regulatory Authority vs. Vendor Influence. Weakness: The options fail to account for the bureaucratic delays in regulatory implementation timelines.

name: Data Sovereignty Enforcement Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Mandating data residency for authentication telemetry → Systemic: 20% reduction in foreign data exposure for critical identity events by 2029 (based on NSIS compliance audits) → Strategic: Transforms identity infrastructure into a national data-control asset rather than a foreign-dependent service.
options: Implement decentralized data storage for authentication logs within Danish sovereign cloud infrastructure
Enforce strict data minimization policies for all identity verification workflows
Require third-party audits of foreign platform data-handling practices in public services
review: Controls Data Security vs. Operational Complexity. Weakness: The options understate the technical burden of maintaining decentralized storage for high-availability systems.

name: User Trust Architecture Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Introducing transparent authentication risk disclosures for citizens → Systemic: 15% rise in public trust scores for digital identity systems by 2027 (based on Danish Digital Society surveys) → Strategic: Positions platform neutrality as a civic trust imperative rather than a technical footnote.
options: Publish annual transparency reports on platform dependency risks for identity services
Design citizen-facing dashboards for authentication method reliability metrics
Integrate user consent management into all identity verification workflows
review: Controls Public Perception vs. Technical Simplicity. Weakness: The options neglect the complexity of translating technical risks into accessible citizen communication.

name: EU Interoperability Advocacy Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Aligning Danish fallback requirements with European Digital Identity Wallet specifications → Systemic: 50% faster EU-wide adoption of platform-neutral standards by 2030 (based on ETSI interoperability benchmarks) → Strategic: Leverages supranational momentum to counterbalance domestic platform vendor lobbying.
options: Propose binding EU-wide fallback authentication clauses in Digital Identity Wallet regulations
Host cross-border technical workshops with ETSI and ENISA stakeholders
Develop a shared EU certification framework for alternative identity protocols
review: Controls Regional Influence vs. National Prioritization. Weakness: The options lack mechanisms to ensure Danish-specific needs aren't diluted in EU standardization processes.

name: Technical Workforce Resilience Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Diversifying expertise in platform-neutral authentication development → Systemic: 40% growth in local identity-system engineering capacity by 2028 (based on Danish IT workforce data) → Strategic: Reduces long-term reliance on foreign technical support for identity infrastructure maintenance.
options: Create a national training program for FIDO2/WebAuthn implementation specialists
Form partnerships with Danish universities for identity-system research clusters
Open-source key components of fallback authentication workflows to build community expertise
review: Controls Knowledge Retention vs. Short-Term Costs. Weakness: The options fail to address the resource intensity of sustaining local expertise without vendor partnerships.

name: Legal Resilience Framework Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Creating binding legal mandates for platform-neutral identity protocols → Systemic: 30% reduction in legal loopholes for foreign vendor dominance by 2028 (based on Danish Data Protection Authority audits) → Strategic: Transforms identity infrastructure into a legally enforceable sovereignty asset.
options: Legally mandate audit trails for all platform-specific authentication dependencies
Establish a national legal sandbox for alternative identity protocols
Require public-sector contracts to include exit-clause penalties for platform lock-in
review: Controls Legal Enforcement vs. Vendor Lobbying. Weakness: The options understate the risk of legal challenges from platform vendors seeking to invalidate mandates.

name: Cross-Border Compliance Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Harmonizing Danish identity requirements with EU-wide digital sovereignty frameworks → Systemic: 25% faster EU adoption of platform-neutral standards by 2029 (based on eIDAS2 alignment metrics) → Strategic: Leverages supranational momentum to counter domestic platform vendor influence.
options: Adopt EU Digital Identity Wallet requirements as de facto national standards
Create a bilateral compliance review mechanism with Nordic partners
Integrate ETSI interoperability benchmarks into public-sector procurement
review: Controls Regional Alignment vs. National Autonomy. Weakness: The options lack mechanisms to protect Danish-specific needs during EU standardization.

name: Public-Private Standards Collaboration Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Formalizing joint development of open identity protocols with private-sector stakeholders → Systemic: 50% increase in industry-led platform-neutral solutions by 2028 (based on Danish IT association surveys) → Strategic: Creates a self-sustaining ecosystem for alternative identity models.
options: Launch a public-private working group for FIDO2 extension standards
Incentivize private-sector R&D through tax credits for open-protocol adoption
Establish a shared patent pool for platform-neutral authentication technologies
review: Controls Industry Collaboration vs. Vendor Capture. Weakness: The options fail to address power imbalances between small innovators and large platform vendors.

name: Decentralized Authentication Development Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Prioritizing decentralized identity architectures over centralized platform dependencies → Systemic: 40% reduction in single-point failure risks for public services by 2029 (based on DRaaS resilience metrics) → Strategic: Reduces long-term vulnerability to platform policy changes or geopolitical disruptions.
options: Scale blockchain-based identity verification for high-risk public services
Develop a decentralized credential storage protocol compliant with ETSI EN 319 401
Create a public repository for open-source decentralized authentication libraries
review: Controls Technical Innovation vs. Adoption Barriers. Weakness: The options neglect the user experience trade-offs of decentralized systems.

name: Regional Infrastructure Diversification Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Expanding identity infrastructure beyond Copenhagen-based systems → Systemic: 20% increase in regional digital sovereignty capacity by 2028 (based on Danish IT infrastructure audits) → Strategic: Mitigates concentration risks by distributing identity infrastructure across multiple geographic nodes.
options: Establish regional identity verification hubs in major Danish cities
Adopt edge-computing architectures for localized authentication
Create a national backup infrastructure fund for identity systems
review: Controls Geographic Resilience vs. Resource Allocation. Weakness: The options understate the logistical challenges of decentralizing infrastructure.
